description_long: This is a large, rustic barn with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles, featuring a prominent stone chimney and a small arched window on one side. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks on the upper half and stone masonry on the lower half, giving it a sturdy, traditional appearance. A wooden water wheel is attached to one side, suggesting the barn may have once served a dual purpose, possibly as a mill. Several rectangular windows with white trim are symmetrically placed along the sides, and a long wooden fence extends from one end. The barn includes a covered entryway and a large main door, likely for vehicle or livestock access. The overall structure is robust and utilitarian, typical of agricultural buildings found in rural or peri-urban settings.
description: A large, traditional barn with a gable roof, stone base, wooden siding, and a water wheel attached to one side. Features multiple windows, a main entry, and a long wooden fence.
description_view_0: Side view showing the water wheel, covered entryway, and several windows with white trim.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entry, stone chimney, arched window, and the start of the wooden fence.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the plain wooden and stone siding, with the fence extending outward.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the full expanse of the gable roof, the back wall, and the water wheel structure.
attribute_siding_pattern: horizontal wooden planks over stone masonry base
attribute_door_style: single large hinged barn door with covered entryway
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple rectangular windows with white trim, symmetrically placed along the sides and a single arched window on the roof side
attribute_architectural_style: traditional American barn with mill features
